title: Quick Fungal DNA Extraction
steps: Inoculate fungal material into 300 µL rich growth medium in a 2 mL micro-centrifuge tube. Grow at RT for 3-4 days. 96:00:00

Freeze culture tubes in LN2 Freeze dry over night 24:00:00 Open the tubes. Obviously. Optional: Place frozen tube in tube rack inside a paper bag to prevent samples flying away when vacuum is released.
Add 50 µL 0.6mm glass beads to freeze-dried sample in 2 mL tube and grind using Tissue-Lyser LT for 5 mins at 50 Hz
Add 500 µL lysis buffer to pulverised sample. Vortex to mix Fungal DNA Lysis Buffer by Adam Taranto
Heat to 45 ˚C for 5 mins, shaking at 1200 rpm. 00:10:00 Alternatively: Shaking rack on max for 10 mins @ RT

Add 0.5 vol (250 µL) 3 M Potassium Acetate to lysis mix. Vortex to mix. Potassium salt of SDS is highly insoluble. Will precipitate SDS and protein in the absence of ethanol. 3 M Potassium Acetate, pH 5.5 Dissolve 29.445 g Potassium Acetate in 80 mL ddH 2 O, pH to 5.5 with 10 M HCl Mass: 98.15 g/mol Target working concentration: 0.3 M
Centrifuge at 12 K rpm for a minimum of 15 minutes @ 4˚C. 00:15:00 Proteins should be precipitated.
Transfer supernatant (~ 650 µL) to fresh 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.
Add 1 vol chilled Isopropanol ( ~ 700 µL ) to the supernatant, mix well, incubate -20˚C for 1hr. 01:00:00
Centrifuge at 12 K rpm for a minimum of 15 minutes at 4°C. Decant gently or aspirate supernatant. 00:15:00 Take care not to dislodge DNA pellet
To the pellet add 500 µL 70% ethanol stored at -20°C.
Centrifuge at 12 K rpm for a minimum of 3 minutes at 4°C. Discard supernatant.
Repeat wash step with 500 µL of cold 70% ethanol. Spin and discard supernatant. 00:05:00
Dry pellet in speed-vac at 45˚C for 5-10 minutes. 00:10:00 Or until all ethanol is evaporated.

Re-suspend pellet in nuclease free water or TE buffer (10 mM Tris pH 8.0, 1 mM EDTA pH 8.0). Some samples may require heated shaking (45˚C @ 1200rpm for 10 mins ) followed by manual pipetting to fully suspend pellet. Especially in polysaccharide-rich samples.

Add 50 µL 3M Sodium Acetate pH 5.5 3 M Sodium Acetate, pH 5.5 Dissolve 24.6103 g Sodium acetate in 80 mL ddH 2 O pH to 5.5 with 10 M HCl Vol to 100 mL Mass: 82.0343 g/mol Target working concentration: 0.3 M Sodium ions re-solubilise SDS to prevent excessive precipitation in DNA pellet
creator: Adam Taranto
